reasoning: The Lean theorem universally quantifies over `p : ℕ` (prime) and `x : ℤ`, with hypothesis `(p : ℤ) ∣ (x^4 - x^2 + 1)`, and concludes `(p : ℤ) ≡ 1 [ZMOD 12]`. This matches the informal statement: "any prime divisor of x^4 - x^2 + 1 is ≡ 1 (mod 12)" is standardly read as "for all integers x and all primes p, if p | x^4 - x^2 + 1 then p ≡ 1 (mod 12)". The ZMOD notation corresponds to `Int.ModEq 12`, i.e., 12 ∣ (p - 1), which exactly expresses "p ≡ 1 modulo 12". Coercing the Nat prime to ℤ for the divisibility and congruence is standard. No hypotheses are added or dropped; the quantifier structure and conclusion match precisely.
